commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: Control UI: keep WebChat composer above iOS keyboard (#36488)
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem: On iOS Safari/WebView, the WebChat composer can be covered by the on-screen keyboard, making the input field hard to see while typing.
- Why it matters: This degrades core chat usability on iOS and can block users from composing messages reliably.
- What changed: Added `visualViewport`-based keyboard inset tracking and exposed it via `--visual-viewport-keyboard-inset`; applied that inset to composer bottom spacing; added `interactive-widget=resizes-content` to the viewport meta.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No gateway/orchestration/auth/model/command logic changed; this PR is limited to Control UI WebChat layout/lifecycle behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #36488
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: The bottom composer UI was effectively anchored to the layout viewport, while iOS keyboard behavior reduces/shifts the visual viewport, so the input could end up behind the keyboard.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No `window.visualViewport` listener or keyboard inset CSS variable existed to keep composer spacing synchronized with viewport changes.
- Contributing context (if known): iOS WebKit handling of fixed/bottom-anchored elements with virtual keyboards differs from desktop browser behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/visual-viewport-keyboard.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Correct keyboard inset calculation for visual viewport resize/offset cases, including non-negative clamping.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The critical logic is a deterministic inset calculation; a focused unit test provides stable coverage without requiring flaky mobile runtime integration.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A (new unit tests were added).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- On iOS Safari/WebView, the WebChat composer remains visible above the virtual keyboard when focused.
- On browsers that support it, `interactive-widget=resizes-content` improves keyboard/layout resize behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
Before:
[user taps composer on iOS] -> [keyboard opens] -> [composer can stay too low] -> [input obscured]

After:
[user taps composer on iOS] -> [visualViewport updates]
-> [compute/set --visual-viewport-keyboard-inset]
-> [composer bottom spacing increases] -> [input remains visible]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `N/A`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: iOS (Safari/WebView target behavior), Linux dev environment for code changes
- Runtime/container: Browser UI (Control UI WebChat)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat
- Relevant config (redacted): Default Control UI/WebChat configuration

### Steps

1. Open WebChat on iOS Safari or iOS WebView.
2. Tap the message composer to open the virtual keyboard.
3. Type into the composer and observe whether the input stays visible.

### Expected

- Composer remains visible and usable above the keyboard.

### Actual

- Before fix: composer could be obscured by the keyboard.
- After fix: composer is lifted via viewport keyboard inset and remains visible.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Implemented and validated keyboard inset logic; added unit tests for inset computation and edge cases.
- Edge cases checked: Positive offset handling; clamping to non-negative values; no-op behavior when `visualViewport` is unavailable.
- What you did **not** verify: Full manual iOS device Safari/WebView run with recording in this environment.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: `N/A`

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Some browsers may emit frequent viewport events, potentially causing minor layout churn.
  - Mitigation: Keep logic lightweight (CSS var update only), clamp values, and uninstall listeners on disconnect.
- Risk: `interactive-widget=resizes-content` is unsupported on some clients.
  - Mitigation: Treated as progressive enhancement; core behavior is handled by `visualViewport` + CSS inset fallback.
target.author: zehanxu-doncic

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
